# US Interconnection Queue Tracker

> A free tool from Kardashev Labs providing unified search across all 7 major US ISO/RTO generator interconnection queues: ERCOT, MISO, PJM, CAISO, SPP, NYISO, and ISO-NE. Tracks every power generation project waiting to connect to the US grid.

The interconnection queue is the list of power generation and storage projects (solar, wind, gas, battery storage, etc.) that have applied to connect to a regional grid operator's transmission system. Projects can wait years before being approved, and queue size/composition is a leading indicator of where new generation capacity is headed. This tool aggregates that data across every major US ISO/RTO into one searchable interface.

## What the data means

- **Project**: A single proposed generation or storage facility with an interconnection request filed with an ISO/RTO.
- **Requested MW**: The nameplate capacity (megawatts) the project has requested to interconnect. This is not guaranteed capacity — many projects in the queue are withdrawn or downsized before completion.
- **Fuel type / technology**: The generation source (solar, wind, battery storage, natural gas, etc.) proposed for the project.
- **Queue status**: Where the project sits in the ISO/RTO's interconnection study and approval process (e.g., active, withdrawn, in service).
- **MW totals** shown in aggregate views (by ISO, fuel type, or status) sum the requested capacity of all matching projects — they represent proposed, not built, capacity.

## Coverage

ERCOT (Texas), MISO (Midcontinent), PJM (Mid-Atlantic/Midwest), CAISO (California), SPP (Southwest Power Pool), NYISO (New York), and ISO-NE (New England). Data is refreshed daily via automated pipelines.

## Key facts

- Free and open source
- Source code: https://github.com/kardashev-lab
- Part of Kardashev Labs: https://kardashevlabs.org
- Shared data platform: https://data.kardashevlabs.org (kardashev-data)
- Contact: ashutosh@kardashevlabs.org

## About Kardashev Labs

Kardashev Labs builds free, open-source tools for US energy grid intelligence, accelerating humanity toward Kardashev Type I by making grid data universally accessible.
